page: Amazon Web Services m5.2xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor operating at 3.1 GHz. The configuration provides 8 dedicated vCPUs derived from 4 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It includes 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, translating to 4.0 GB of RAM per core. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, and network connectivity is rated at a baseline of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate average multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth. The stress-ng multi-core CPU test and Passmark CPU score both fall within the middle 50% tier. Similarly, memory bandwidth tests for both cached and uncached reads and writes show average performance. However, single-core capabilities are weak, with stress-ng single-core, Geekbench single-core, and single-threaded compression tests scoring in the bottom 25%. LLM inference speeds for a small 135M model are average, but drop to poor or weak levels for medium 7B models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m5.2xlarge offers a standard, balanced resource density of 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM, making it a qualitative baseline for general-purpose computing. The primary tradeoffs include the lack of local storage and weak single-threaded execution speeds. This server is best suited for workloads that benefit from balanced resources rather than raw single-core speed, such as software compiling, static web serving, Redis database operations, and general-purpose application hosting.
description: Amazon Web Services m5.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 8 dedicated vCPUs and 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 server provides a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio but lacks local storage and has a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Performance benchmarks show average multi-core CPU capabilities, memory bandwidth, and static web serving throughput, though single-core performance and single-threaded compression tasks fall into the bottom 25%. Qualitative cost efficiency is aligned with standard general-purpose resource allocation. The instance is suited for workloads such as software compiling, Redis database operations, static web serving, and general-purpose application hosting where balanced compute and memory are required without demanding high single-core performance.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose server offering dedicated x86_64 processing and standard memory density for multi-core workloads.
